what: Simulate and fit a generalized linear mixed model
title: Lanczos for penalized likelihood models
text: first simulate data compound lognormal-Gamma process: define function compute joint likelihood, also allowing us calculate outputs later use: Finally, fit model log-linked generalized linear mixed model (GLMM):
code: set.seed(123) n = 30 n_sum = 3  # log-linked normal deviates u = 0 + rnorm(n)  # Conditional gamma process y = rgamma( n, shape = 1/0.5^2, scale = exp(u) * 0.5^2 ) nll = function(p){   sumexpu = sum(exp(p$u[seq_len(n_sum)]))   ADREPORT( sumexpu )   REPORT( sumexpu )   nll1 = dnorm(p$u, mean=p$mu, sd=exp(p$logsd), log=TRUE)   nll2 = dgamma(      y,      shape = 1/exp(2*p$logcv),      scale = exp(p$u) * exp(2*p$logcv),      log=TRUE   )   jnll = -1 * ( sum(nll1) + sum(nll2) )   if(what == "jnll") return(jnll)   if(what == "sumexpu") return(sumexpu)   if(what == "biascorr") return(jnll + p$eps * sumexpu) } # Build RTMB object what = "jnll" obj = RTMB::MakeADFun(    nll,    list(u=u, mu = 0, logsd = 0, logcv = 0, eps = 0),   map = list(eps = factor(NA)),   random = "u",   silent = TRUE )  # optimize  opt = nlminb( obj$par, obj$fn, obj$gr ) opt #> $par #>         mu      logsd      logcv  #> -0.1052225 -0.1326400 -0.5984998  #>  #> $objective #> [1] 36.46907 #>  #> $convergence #> [1] 0 #>  #> $iterations #> [1] 13 #>  #> $evaluations #> function gradient  #>       17       14  #>  #> $message #> [1] "relative convergence (4)"

what: Fit as a penalized likelihood model
title: Lanczos for penalized likelihood models
text: Next, show model can refitted using penalized likelihood, conditional upon fixed values variance parameters: can use stochastic trace estimation Lanczos method approximate log-determinant Hessian random effects: Using log-determinant, can also compare log-marginal likelihood using Lanczos full calculation:
code: # Define RTMB object for penalized likelihood newmap = list(   mu = factor(NA),    logsd = factor(NA),    logcv = factor(NA),   eps = factor(NA) ) pen = RTMB::MakeADFun(    nll,    obj$env$parList(),    map = newmap,   silent = TRUE )  # Re-optimize opt_pen = nlminb( pen$par, pen$fn, pen$gr ) # log-determinant using Lanczos Hv = make_Hv(pen) lanczos_logdet( Hv, k = 10, m = 3, n = length(pen$par) ) #> [1] 44.4956 44.4956 44.4956  # log-determinant for marginal likelihood H = obj$env$spHess(par = obj$env$last.par.best, random = TRUE) Matrix::determinant( H )$modulus #> [1] 44.4956 #> attr(,"logarithm") #> [1] TRUE # Marginal likelihoods using Lanczos lanczos_nll( pen, k = 10, m = 10 ) #>      nll   sd_nll  #> 36.46907  0.00000  # Marginal likelihood using Laplace opt$obj #> [1] 36.46907

title: Lanczos for penalized likelihood models
text: Alternatively, can apply Lanczos methods standard errors parameters. , sample first three random effects Alternatively, can apply Lanczos methods approximate Hessian respect derived quantity. involves calculating gradient derived quantity respect coefficients: can use gradient approximate samples Hessian matrix use calculate Monte Carlo estimator standard errors: Alternatively, can calculate standard error derived quantity using Lanczos within delta method:
code: samples = lanczos_sample(    Hv = Hv,    v = c( rep(1,3), rep(0,n-3) ),    k = 30,    n = 1000,    orthogonalize = TRUE )  # sdrep = sdreport( obj, ignore.parm.uncertainty = TRUE ) as.list(sdrep, what = "Std. Error")$u[1:3] #> [1] 0.4839019 0.4864751 0.4066186 apply( samples, MARGIN = 1, FUN = sd)[1:3] #> [1] 0.4836432 0.4823133 0.4087638 what = "sumexpu" grad = RTMB::MakeADFun(    nll,    obj$env$parList(),    map = newmap,   silent = TRUE )$gr( pen$par )[1,] samples = lanczos_sample(    Hv = Hv,    v = grad,    k = 30,    n = 1000,    orthogonalize = TRUE )  # Compare SD of samples with the delta method sumexpu1 = apply( samples, MARGIN = 2, FUN = \(v)pen$report(v)$sumexpu ) c( pen$report()$sumexpu, sd(sumexpu1) ) #> [1] 4.064121 0.974645 summary(sdrep)['sumexpu',] #>   Estimate Std. Error  #>   4.064121   1.194484 #  Var = lanczos_variance(   Hv = Hv,   v = grad,   k = 3 )  # Compare with the delta method c( pen$report()$sumexpu, sqrt(Var) ) #> [1] 4.064121 1.194484 summary(sdrep)['sumexpu',] #>   Estimate Std. Error  #>   4.064121   1.194484

title: Lanczos for penalized likelihood models
text: Finally, can use gradient log-likelihood respect dummy variable epsilon correct retransformation bias: Runtime vignette: 2.34 secs
code: # One-sided finite difference what = "biascorr" phat = obj$env$parList() phat$eps = 0.0001 pen_hi = RTMB::MakeADFun(    nll,    parameters = phat,    map = newmap,   silent = TRUE )  # Re-optimize opt_hi = optim(    pen_hi$par, pen_hi$fn, pen_hi$gr,    method = "L-BFGS-B", control = list(factr = 1e-2)  )  # Calculate marginal nll for finite-difference nll_hi = lanczos_nll( pen_hi, k = 10, m = 10, seed = 123 ) nll_mid = lanczos_nll( pen, k = 10, m = 10, seed = 123 )  # Epsilon bias-correction estimator sdrep = sdreport( obj, bias.correct = TRUE )  # (nll_hi['nll'] - nll_mid['nll']) / (phat$eps) #>      nll  #> 4.733913 summary(sdrep)['sumexpu',] #>            Estimate          Std. Error Est. (bias.correct) Std. (bias.correct)  #>            4.064121            1.625808            4.734025                  NA

title: Lanczos methods for mixed-effects models in RTMB
text: Lanczos methods RTMB

title: Lanczos methods for mixed-effects models in RTMB
text: lanczosRTMB designed approximate properties hierarchical model implemented RTMB without ever constructing inverting matrix second derivatives (“Hessian matrix”). can used approximate: Standard errors using matrix-free delta method log-determinant Hessian using stochastic trace estimation log marginal likelihood using Laplace approximation

title: Assemble tri-diagonal matrix — lanczos
text: Assemble tri-diagonal matrix alpha beta Lanczos method

title: Assemble tri-diagonal matrix — lanczos
code: lanczos(Hv, q1, k, orthogonalize = FALSE, tol = 1e-12)

title: Assemble tri-diagonal matrix — lanczos
text: Hv function calculates product Hv q1 initial vector used defining Kyrlov subspace k dimension Kyrlov subspace orthogonalize Whether two-pass Gram-Schmidt re-normalization (much slower) tol numerical tolerance stopping algorithm given terms identifiable

title: Assemble tri-diagonal matrix — lanczos
code: H = diag(exp(rnorm(5))) v = rep(1,5) Hv = function(v) (H %*% v)[,1]  L = lanczos(Hv, v, k = 5, ortho = TRUE) T = tridiag(L$alpha, L$beta)  # Should match H if and only if L$m = nrow(H) range(L$Q %*% T %*% t(L$Q) - H) #> [1] -3.560819e-16  2.972869e-16

what: Approximate log-marginal likelihood using Lanczos method (EXPERIMENTAL) — lanczos_MakeADFun
title: Approximate log-marginal likelihood using Lanczos method (EXPERIMENTAL) — lanczos_MakeADFun
text: Make function returns Lanczos-Laplace approximation user-supplied joint likelihood designated random effects

title: Approximate log-marginal likelihood using Lanczos method (EXPERIMENTAL) — lanczos_MakeADFun
code: lanczos_MakeADFun(   func,   parameters,   random,   k,   profile = NULL,   m = 3,   seed = 123 )

title: Approximate log-marginal likelihood using Lanczos method (EXPERIMENTAL) — lanczos_MakeADFun
text: func Function taking parameter list (parameter vector) input. parameters Parameter list (parameter vector) used func. random MakeADFun. k dimension Kyrlov subspace profile MakeADFun. m number probe-vectors use approximating average standard deviation log-determinant seed NULL, sets seed.  helfpul given Hutchinson probe vectors randomly sampled, comparisons lower variance using fixed seed.

title: Approximate log-marginal likelihood using Lanczos method (EXPERIMENTAL) — lanczos_MakeADFun
text: object (list) class tinyVAST. Elements include: par parameter-vector fixed effects fn function returns Lanczos-Laplace approximation given fixed effects env environment local variables Hv function returns Hessian-vector product (use debugging)

what: Estimate log-determinant likelihood using Lanczos method — lanczos_logdet
title: Estimate log-determinant likelihood using Lanczos method — lanczos_logdet
text: Estimate log-determinant inner Hessian matrix stochastic approximation

title: Estimate log-determinant likelihood using Lanczos method — lanczos_logdet
code: lanczos_logdet(Hv, k, m, n, seed = NULL, orthogonalize = TRUE)

title: Estimate log-determinant likelihood using Lanczos method — lanczos_logdet
text: Hv function calculates product Hv k dimension Kyrlov subspace m number probe-vectors use approximating average standard deviation log-determinant n length parameters (necessary probe-vector) seed NULL, sets seed.  helfpul given Hutchinson probe vectors randomly sampled, comparisons lower variance using fixed seed. orthogonalize Whether two-pass Gram-Schmidt re-normalization (much slower)

title: Estimate log-determinant likelihood using Lanczos method — lanczos_logdet
text: model independent random effects, variance stochastic trace estimation zero

what: Estimate log-marginal likelihood using Lanczos method — lanczos_nll
title: Estimate log-marginal likelihood using Lanczos method — lanczos_nll
text: Estimate log-marginal likelihood using Laplace approximation, replacing exact calculation log-determinant stochastic approximation

title: Estimate log-marginal likelihood using Lanczos method — lanczos_nll
code: lanczos_nll(obj, k, m, Hv, seed = NULL)

title: Estimate log-marginal likelihood using Lanczos method — lanczos_nll
text: obj TMB object (output TMB::MakeADFun) k dimension Kyrlov subspace m number probe-vectors use approximating average standard deviation log-determinant Hv function calculates product Hv seed NULL, sets seed.  helfpul given Hutchinson probe vectors randomly sampled, comparisons lower variance using fixed seed.

title: Estimate log-marginal likelihood using Lanczos method — lanczos_nll
code: # Simulate lognormal-gamma process set.seed(123) library(RTMB) n = 30 n_sum = 3 u = 0 + rnorm(n) y = rgamma( n, shape = 1/0.5^2, scale = exp(u) * 0.5^2 )  # Fit as GLMM what = "jnll" nll = function(p){   sumexpu = sum(exp(p$u[seq_len(n_sum)]))   ADREPORT( sumexpu )   REPORT( sumexpu )   nll1 = dnorm(p$u, mean=p$mu, sd=exp(p$logsd), log=TRUE)   nll2 = dgamma(y, shape = 1/exp(2*p$logcv), scale = exp(p$u) * exp(2*p$logcv), log=TRUE)   jnll = -1 * ( sum(nll1) + sum(nll2) )   if(what == "jnll") return(jnll)   if(what == "sumexpu") return(sumexpu) } obj = RTMB::MakeADFun( nll, list(u=u, mu = 0, logsd = 0, logcv = 0), random = "u" ) opt = nlminb( obj$par, obj$fn, obj$gr ) #> iter: 1  value: 58.37851 mgc: 2.856111 ustep: 1  #> iter: 2  value: 57.98311 mgc: 0.6945181 ustep: 1  #> iter: 3  value: 57.98169 mgc: 0.04932098 ustep: 1  #> iter: 4  value: 57.98169 mgc: 0.0002842405 ustep: 1  #> iter: 5  mgc: 9.465661e-09  #> iter: 1  mgc: 9.465661e-09  #> Matching hessian patterns... code: # Simulate lognormal-gamma process set.seed(123) library(RTMB) n = 30 n_sum = 3 u = 0 + rnorm(n) y = rgamma( n, shape = 1/0.5^2, scale = exp(u) * 0.5^2 )  # Fit as GLMM what = "jnll" nll = function(p){   sumexpu = sum(exp(p$u[seq_len(n_sum)]))   ADREPORT( sumexpu )   REPORT( sumexpu )   nll1 = dnorm(p$u, mean=p$mu, sd=exp(p$logsd), log=TRUE)   nll2 = dgamma(y, shape = 1/exp(2*p$logcv), scale = exp(p$u) * exp(2*p$logcv), log=TRUE)   jnll = -1 * ( sum(nll1) + sum(nll2) )   if(what == "jnll") return(jnll)   if(what == "sumexpu") return(sumexpu) } obj = RTMB::MakeADFun( nll, list(u=u, mu = 0, logsd = 0, logcv = 0), random = "u" ) opt = nlminb( obj$par, obj$fn, obj$gr ) #> iter: 1  value: 58.37851 mgc: 2.856111 ustep: 1  #> iter: 2  value: 57.98311 mgc: 0.6945181 ustep: 1  #> iter: 3  value: 57.98169 mgc: 0.04932098 ustep: 1  #> iter: 4  value: 57.98169 mgc: 0.0002842405 ustep: 1  #> iter: 5  mgc: 9.465661e-09  #> iter: 1  mgc: 9.465661e-09  #> Matching hessian patterns... what: Estimate variance using Lanczos method — lanczos_variance
title: Estimate variance using Lanczos method — lanczos_variance
text: Estimate squared standard error (variance) parameter derived quantity without forming Hessian matrix directly

title: Estimate variance using Lanczos method — lanczos_variance
code: lanczos_variance(   Hv,   v,   k = c(25, 30),   min_spectral_ratio = 1e-10,   orthogonalize = FALSE )

title: Estimate variance using Lanczos method — lanczos_variance
text: Hv function calculates product Hv v vector use calculating variance, either indicator single parameter, gradient evaluated MLE derived quantity k can vector min_spectral_ratio ratio minimum maximum ratio, values < minimum truncated minimum (min_spectral_ratio=0 disables truncation) orthogonalize Whether two-pass Gram-Schmidt re-normalization (much slower)

what: Make function to calculate H %*% v — make_Hv
title: Make function to calculate H %*% v — make_Hv
text: Given TMB object, make function efficiently calculates H %*% v without constructing H , instead using grad_x( grad_x(f) %** v) given function f(x) returns negative log-likelihood.  can used e.g. Lanczos methods H large construct explicitly

title: Make function to calculate H %*% v — make_Hv
Make function to calculate H %*% v — make_Hv
text: obj TMB object (output TMB::MakeADFun) par parameter vector p used evaluating H

title: Make function to calculate H %*% v — make_Hv
code: u = rnorm(100) y = rpois(length(u), exp(u)) nll = function(p) -1 * ( sum(dnorm(p$u,log=TRUE)) + sum(dpois(y,exp(p$u),log=TRUE)) ) obj = RTMB::MakeADFun( nll, list(u=u) ) Hv = make_Hv( obj ) # Confirm v = rnorm( length(obj$par) ) all.equal( Hv(v)[1,], (obj$he()%*%v)[,1] ) #> [1] TRUE

title: Assemble tri-diagonal symmetric matrix — tridiag
text: Assemble tri-diagonal matrix alpha beta Lanczos method

Assemble tri-diagonal symmetric matrix — tridiag
